metadata.name: Multiple blockers — attacker assigns damage in declared order
metadata.description: p1 attacks with Darksteel Colossus (11/11, trample). p2 blocks with both Llanowar Elves (1/1) and Elvish Mystic (1/1). p1 must assign damage in the declared order: at least lethal to each blocker before assigning to the next. With trample, excess damage goes to p2. Tests CR 510.1c multi-blocker damage assignment.
metadata.generation_notes: CR 510.1c: When multiple creatures block a single attacker, the attacking player assigns damage in a declared order. CR 510.1d: Attacker must assign at least lethal damage to each blocker before moving to the next. With trample, remaining damage goes to defending player.

script.step_note: Darksteel Colossus (11/11 trample) attacks; both p2 creatures block.

actions.description: Initial: Darksteel Colossus vs two 1/1 blockers, p2 at 40
Declare order: p1 orders Llanowar Elves first, Elvish Mystic second
actions.note: CR 509.2: When multiple creatures block one attacker, the attacker declares the damage assignment order.

actions.description: Damage assignment: 1 to Llanowar Elves, 1 to Elvish Mystic, 9 trample to p2
actions.note: CR 510.1c: 1 damage to Llanowar Elves (lethal for 1/1), 1 damage to Elvish Mystic (lethal), 9 excess trample damage to p2. p2: 40 - 9 = 31.

actions.description: SBA: both blockers have lethal damage
